hypotheses.description: GitHub v0.1.5 is not actually the latest release exposed to the client
The rebuilt v0.1.4 installer asset is secretly embedding APP_VERSION 0.1.5
The rebuilt v0.1.4 installer asset should detect v0.1.5 correctly under the current update-check logic
The user's local runtime is not the same binary as the current GitHub v0.1.4 asset

evidence.note: User successfully reproduced update detection and reached installer-based upgrade flow, so the launched v0.1.4 binary matches the rebuilt asset closely enough for update detection.

hypotheses.description: The rebuilt v0.1.5 executable is itself corrupted and cannot start even outside the installer flow
The frozen updater leaks PyInstaller DLL search state and environment into the installer, which then relaunches the new frozen app under a poisoned runtime environment

evidence.user_error: Failed to load Python DLL after installer-based upgrade from running frozen app
evidence.official_guidance: PyInstaller common issues docs recommend clearing SetDllDirectoryW before launching external programs from a frozen Windows app
evidence.fix: sanitize DLL search path, PATH, _PYI_* variables, _MEIPASS2, and bundled TCL/TK environment before os.startfile(installer)
